title: Regulatory Support Administrator
description:
We are an Australian-based fragrance and lifestyle business supplying products globally across home fragrance, personal care, and FMCG markets.



We are looking for a highly organised and detail-oriented Regulatory Support Administrator to assist with managing and issuing technical documentation required for product compliance.



This is a remote contract role, working with an international team and aligned to Australian business hours.



Key Responsibilities:

• Manage a shared regulatory inbox and respond to documentation requests.

• Prepare and issue regulatory documents including:

Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

IFRA Certificates

Allergen Declarations

Technical Data Sheets (TDS)

Certificates of Analysis (CoA)

• Maintain accurate document control, versioning, and filing systems.

• Track and manage product compliance documentation across multiple products.

• Liaise with internal teams (technical, sales, operations).

• Support regulatory workflows and process improvements.



Required Experience:

• 2+ years experience in administration, compliance, or technical documentation roles.

• Strong experience with document control and structured workflows.

• Excellent written English and communication skills.

• High attention to detail and accuracy.

• Ability to manage multiple requests and deadlines.



Highly Regarded (Not Essential):

• Experience with SDS, IFRA, or regulatory documentation.

• Background in cosmetics, fragrance, chemical, or FMCG industries.



Working Structure:

• Remote contract role.

• Must be available during Australian Eastern Time (AEST) business hours.

• Initial 3-month contract to assess fit, with strong potential for ongoing work for the right candidate.



Application Instructions:

To apply, please follow these instructions carefully:

1. Begin your application with:

"Regulatory Support Pro"

2. Include the following:

• A link to your resume.

• Your relevant experience with documentation, compliance, or admin roles.

• Any experience with SDS, IFRA, or regulatory documentation (if applicable).

• Your availability in AEST hours.

• A short example of a process or system you have managed or improved.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Subject: High-Energy Growth Partner | Expert at Scaling AI SaaS for Contractors
description:
The Mission

Most roofing contractors are drowning in paperwork. CompanySync changes that with a 60-second AI satellite estimator. We aren't looking for "workers"; we are looking for Growth Partners who can command a conversation with American business owners and show them the future of their industry.



Why This is Different

The Tech: You aren't selling "leads." You’re demoing a cutting-edge AI (Lexi) that actually solves a contractor's biggest headache.



The Pay: This is built for earners. While there is a weekly retainer, the real money is in the $75 per subscriber conversion. If you can close, you will make more here in 10 hours than most make in 40.



The Autonomy: No rigid scripts. You need to be smart enough to handle objections on the fly and "vibe" with blue-collar owners.



Who You Are

Elite Communication: Your English is near-native/neutral. You can hold a professional, high-energy conversation without sounding like a telemarketer.



Tech-Fluent: You can navigate a CRM, record personalized Loom videos, and run a smooth Zoom demo without breaking a sweat.



The "Shine": You have a personality that cuts through the noise. When you send a video to a lead, they have to click it.



Compensation Structure (The "Hustler" Model)

$100/Week Retainer: Paid for meeting baseline outreach volume.



$15 Per Qualified Trial: Every time a user runs an AI estimate.



$75 Per Paid Conversion: When that trial turns into a monthly subscriber.



? MANDATORY APPLICATION STEP

Do not just click apply. We only hire people who can sell.

Send us a 1-minute video (Loom or YouTube).



Briefly pitch yourself.



Tell us why you're the right person to sell AI to a skeptical roofing contractor.

Applications without a video link will be instantly deleted.Applications without a video will be ignored.

title: Virtual Assistant (Quality Control + Project Coordinator)

US-based real estate photography company is looking for a full-time Virtual Assistant to support us with day-to-day operational tasks, including delivering photos & sending invoices, and basic Photoshop editing.



We are looking for someone who is smart, organized, reliable, efficient, friendly, motivated, and excited to grow with us! If you’re a positive, detail-oriented, hard-working person who loves to learn, takes feedback well, and wants to be part of a supportive team — we’d love to meet you.



This is a long-term role for someone who’s ready to take on more responsibility over time and become a key part of our journey.



WORK HOURS

Full-time, 2pm to 10pm Pacific Time (5am - 1pm Manila time) is our preferred schedule, but this can be a little bit flexible



STARTING SALARY: $4-5/hour



Who We Are:



Inside Media is a family-run real estate media company based in the U.S. We create beautiful photo and video content for realtors, and we’re known for our fast turnaround, friendly service, and high-quality work. Our team is growing quickly and looking for our next project coordinator to help us maintain our quality and turnaround standards.





Job Summary



Manage, review for quality, upload, and retouch photos on a daily basis. Ensure projects are delivered on time, and follow up with editors as needed. Maintain communication with admin team.



RESPONSIBILITIES



Project Management

Quality Control for Deliverables (Photos, Videos, 3D Tours)

Make edits to photos in Photoshop/Lightroom as needed

Communicate with Admin team on deliveries



SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS



Ability to take on several tasks at once.

Extreme attention to detail.

Excellent written English.

Proficient in Canva, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Lightroom, Slack.

Fast learner and willing to learn

Tech-savvy - comfortable with quickly learning new software

Excellent internet connection (this job is a fully remote position and you will be uploading large files)

Very close attention to detail

Real estate editing experience is a big pro

Graphic design experience is a pro

Basic video editing skills is a pro 



INSTRUCTIONS TO APPLY



Please
 
 your resume along with a video of you answering the following questions:



1. Please tell us a little bit about you personally. What do you love?



2. What makes you the perfect candidate to join our team?



3. Please detail your past work experience and how it relates to this job. 



4. What about this job posting stood out to you the most ? What made you excited about applying?



This does not need to be a fancy video - a quick phone video is great!



Please make the subject line: "VA Position - [Your Name]" without quotation marks or brackets, so we know you have read this job description in full.



Thank you!

title: Work-from-Home Opportunity with Australian Criminal Law Firm – Long-Term Role | Weekend Shift (Sunday-Thursday)
description:
Work-from-Home Opportunity with Australian Criminal Law Firm – Long-Term Role | Weekend Shift (Sunday- Thursday)



We are a criminal law firm based in Australia, previously working with external call centres, but now building our dedicated in-house remote team. We currently have 5 valued tea
mbers based in the Philippines and are looking to add a Sixth for a long-term position.







Position Overview



This is a home-based, full-time role with the potential to earn up to PHP 100,000/month — starting at PHP 50,000 during training, with a raise to PHP 81,500 post-training, and bonuses up to PHP 10,000 based on commissions.







Schedule & Hours



-Permanent shift: From Sunday- Thursday



-Days off: Friday- Saturday



-11.5 hours per day (including a 45-minute lunch break)



-Working hours: 7:00 AM to approx. 6:30 PM ( There will be a rotation of roster so there are weeks where you only work from 6 AM to 2 PM Philippine time (aligned with Australian business hours and nightphones)



-Training schedule: Monday to Friday until fully trained



- Entitled to 15 vacation leaves







Your Duties Will Include:



-Answering incoming calls and handling client inquiries professionally



-Recording call details and booking appointments in our database



-Learning the basics of criminal law in Australia



-Providing frontline call management and transferring messages



-Managing live chat inquiries



- Collecting payment during after-hrs only



-Assisting with after-hours and weekend nightphones





What We're Looking For:



-Fluent English speaker with strong writing and spelling skills



-Reliable computer, high-speed internet, second monitor, and quality headphones (Bluetooth is preferred)



-Availability for full-time work only — no side jobs or agency representation



-A strong team player who is eager to learn, grow, and contribute long-term



-Willingness to work on Philippine holidays (with holiday pay already factored in)



-Rarely take leaves on a weekend.







Why Join Us?



-Work closely with a team of 35+ Australian lawyers and staff



-Join a growing and stable firm that values its remote team



-Receive thorough training and continuous support



-Enjoy long-term stability — our current PH team has been with us for 3+ years



-We pay the 13th month and offer performance-based bonuses







How to Apply:



-Please submit a voice and video introduction (e.g., via Vocaroo) sharing:



-A short introduction about yourself



-Your relevant experience



-Why you think you’d be a great fit for this role



-Provide your resume as well.



- Provide a typing test



-Only applicants who submit a voice or video introduction will be considered.







Do NOT apply if:



You’re an agency



You plan to take on another job



You don’t meet the equipment or availability requirements



We use real-time communication tools during working hours, and full commitment to this role is essential.







This is a great opportunity to join a respected and fast-growing Australian law firm ( Dribbin & Brown Criminal Lawyers). Before joining, we’re happy to connect you with our current Filipino staff so you can hear firsthand about the role and work environment.



We look forward to hearing from you!

This role ensures that Therapist Mentors deliver high-quality clinical sessions, follow established policies and procedures, and maintain accurate data collection and documentation.

Required Qualifications

Strong communication, observation, and documentation skills.





Ability to provide corrective feedback professionally and confidently.

title: 24 HR Review | VA Remote Virtual Receptionist
description:
Important: Our application process moves quickly. If you are selected to proceed to the next interview stages, you must be able to respond promptly. Candidates who do not respond in a timely manner may be passed over.



We are currently seeking a Remote Spa Receptionist to manage inbound calls and book spa appointments. This is a long-term, performance-based position. Calls are recorded and reviewed regularly. Continued placement in this role depends on accuracy, professionalism, booking success, reliable attendance, no missed calls during scheduled shifts, quick response times, and the ability to professionally manage complaints and de-escalate dissatisfied clients.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.

title: Sales Admin & AI Operations Support
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW



Fintonia Group is seeking a Senior Sales Admin & AI Operations Support professional to serve as the operational backbone of its sales and client engagement functions. This is not a conventional administrative role: the individual will be responsible for maintaining rigorous sales operations while independently building and managing AI-powered workflows that enhance team productivity, client responsiveness, and process quality. A non-negotiable attribute for this role is an exceptional standard of accuracy — the work product this person owns directly reflects on the firm's credibility with clients and counterparties.



The successful candidate will bring a combination of structured administrative discipline and a strong command of modern AI automation tools. Reporting directly to the Head of Sales, this person will work across sales, compliance, and operations to ensure that client onboarding and engagement workflows operate to institutional standards.



At a firm that sits at the intersection of traditional finance and digital assets, the ability to translate operational complexity into streamlined, technology-enabled processes will be a direct contribution to commercial outcomes.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. AI Workflow Design & Maintenance

- Own the design, build, and ongoing maintenance of AI-assisted workflows across sales operations, client communications, and document management.

- Identify manual or repetitive processes and deploy automation solutions using tools such as n8n, Zapier, Make, or similar platforms integrated with LLMs.

- Maintain and iterate on prompt libraries, automated response flows, and AI-assisted drafting tools used by the sales team.

- Monitor AI workflow performance, troubleshoot failures, and implement improvements in response to operational feedback.



2. Sales Operations Support

- Manage CRM hygiene and data integrity, ensuring pipeline records, contact logs, and activity tracking remain current and accurate.

- Prepare regular sales reports and performance summaries for the Head of Sales, translating pipeline and activity data into clear, actionable insights.

- Support internal reporting requirements by compiling and structuring data from CRM and other operational systems.



3. Client Onboarding & Coordination

- Manage the preparation and tracking of client onboarding documentation, coordinating with compliance and operations to ensure timely completion.

- Act as a reliable point of contact for client queries, ensuring responses are substantive, accurate, and delivered within agreed timeframes.

- Organize and support client meetings, webinars, and product demonstrations, including logistics, materials, and follow-up coordination.



4. Process Improvement

- Proactively identify bottlenecks in sales and client service workflows and develop structured proposals for improvement.

- Document operational procedures and build internal knowledge bases that reduce dependency on tribal knowledge.



REQUIREMENTS (MUST-HAVE):

- 4+ years of experience in sales support, client operations, or administrative roles within financial services or a related regulated environment.

- Demonstrated hands-on experience building and maintaining AI-assisted workflows using tools such as n8n, Make, Zapier, or equivalent automation platforms.

- Working knowledge of LLM prompt design and practical application of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) in a professional operations context.

- Proficiency in CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) with demonstrated experience maintaining data quality.

- Strong written and verbal communication skills in English; capable of producing client-grade documentation independently.

- Experience with client onboarding documentation or compliance-adjacent processes.

- Exceptional attention to detail — this role involves managing documentation, data, and client records where errors have direct business and reputational consequences. Candidates must demonstrate a track record of precision under workload pressure.

- Ability to work independently in a remote environment, exercise sound judgment, and escalate appropriately.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS (NICE-TO-HAVE):

- Experience building internal tools or documentation systems (e.g., Notion, Confluence, Airtable).

- Background working in or with cross-functional teams spanning compliance, product, and sales.

- Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Finance, Information Systems, or a related discipline.



SUCCESS METRICS (FIRST 6–12 MONTHS):

- At least 3 recurring manual workflows have been replaced or materially accelerated through AI automation, with measurable time savings documented.

- Sales reports are delivered consistently on schedule, with the Head of Sales confirming they are actionable and replacing previously manual consolidation efforts.

- Client onboarding turnaround time meets or improves on established benchmarks, with zero compliance escalations attributed to documentation errors.

- CRM data integrity score (completeness and accuracy) meets the internal standard set by the sales lead.

- Sales team reports a meaningful reduction in administrative overhead, allowing more time to be allocated to client-facing activities.



ABOUT FINTONIA GROUP:

Fintonia Group is a technology-enabled digital assets financial services platform with offices in Singapore and Kuala Lumpur. The firm combines deep institutional finance expertise with a focused mandate to bring credible, regulated financial services infrastructure to the digital assets space.



The team is composed of professionals with backgrounds at leading global investment banks and financial institutions. Fintonia operates in a dynamic, high-accountability environment where individual contributions have direct business impact and where the expectation is ownership, not execution of instructions.



Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Work Hours: Aligned with Singapore Time Zone (SGT), Monday to Friday

ONLY applicants who upload CV via the link will be considered. Thanks!

title: Figma/Framer Parallax Web Designer
description:
We are looking for a talented Web Designer / Front-End Developer to create a modern, high-converting SaaS website with premium visuals, smooth interactions, and PARALLAX SCROLLING EFFECTS.



Examples





The ideal candidate understands how to combine great design, motion, and front-end execution to build a site that feels polished, fast, and conversion-focused.



Responsibilities

Design and build a modern SaaS marketing website

Create visually engaging sections with parallax scrolling, layered motion, and smooth transitions

Develop responsive landing pages that look strong across desktop, tablet, and mobile

Build key website sections such as hero banners, trust/logo walls, feature blocks, statistics, testimonials, and CTA sections

Implement scroll-triggered animations without sacrificing site speed or usability

Translate design concepts into clean, scalable front-end code

Ensure strong UX, readability, and conversion flow throughout the website

Optimize performance, responsiveness, and animation smoothness

Collaborate on layout, messaging hierarchy, and visual storytelling

Requirements

Proven experience designing and/or developing high-end SaaS websites

Strong portfolio showing modern website design and motion-heavy web experiences

Strong skills in Figma

Strong front-end skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript

Experience with React / Next.js is preferred

Experience with animation libraries such as GSAP or Framer Motion

Good understanding of parallax scrolling, sticky sections, and scroll-based interactions

Strong understanding of responsive design and cross-device behavior

Ability to balance aesthetics with speed, clarity, and conversions

Strong eye for typography, spacing, layout, and premium UI details

Nice to Have

Experience building B2B SaaS or tech startup websites

Experience in Webflow or Framer

Understanding of conversion-focused landing page structure

title: $3,000+/Month Part-Time — Only 2 Spots Left

title: Acquisition Specialist – Cold Caller & Closer
description:
Acquisition Specialist – Cold Caller & Closer



(Single Point of Accountability | Performance-Only | Zero Handoffs)



WARNING:

Apply only if you have verified experience in both outbound cold calling and direct-to-seller closing. If you require handoffs, coaching, or flexible standards, this is not for you.



Role Overview



You are solely responsible for generating, qualifying, and closing distressed property deals (unpaid taxes, inheritance, title issues, problem properties).

You will be measured and retained based exclusively on daily call output, pipeline quality, and contracts closed—no exceptions.



Your Responsibilities



100+ live cold call conversations per day (not dials—actual conversations logged and recorded)



Immediate qualification/disqualification using a fixed criteria set—no “relationship building” without outcome



Full-cycle deal management: Personally negotiate, contract, and close each deal—no handoffs at any stage



Accurate, real-time CRM entry for every contact, note, and pipeline movement



Follow strict process: All activity and results tracked and audited. Miss metrics for any reason = immediate removal



Who You Must Be



Proven in both cold calling (high daily volume) and full-cycle closing (real estate or equivalent asset class)



Able to provide daily metrics and references—not negotiable



Rigid about pipeline management, documentation, and transparent reporting



Comfortable with direct feedback and negative coaching—no sensitivity to high-rejection or blunt performance management



Compensation-driven, with a clear history of thriving under performance-only pay plans



Immediate Disqualifiers



No resume or lack of specific metrics for calls/deals



No voice memo, or memo not following exact instructions below



History of team-selling, “split” process roles, or reluctance to own the full sales cycle



Evidence of missed quotas, resistance to feedback, or incomplete reporting



Application Process



1. Submit your resume clearly listing outbound + closing experience, including your highest daily call volume and annual closed deals.

2. Submit a 2–3 minute, unscripted voice memo (no script, no edits) covering, in order:



Your name, country, and highest daily cold call volume in a sales role



An example of a distressed or complicated deal you personally closed (describe steps and outcome)



Your personal system for tracking activity, pipeline, and follow-ups



The most direct/negative feedback you’ve received about your sales process and what changed after



Why you want a single-point, output-only sales role (not team selling or account management)



Any deviation from these steps = immediate rejection.



Do Not Apply If:



You lack both cold calling and direct closing experience with provable metrics



You need daily encouragement, “culture,” or ongoing training



You resist process, documentation, or daily scorecard reporting



You want a collaborative/”team” sales role

title: Web & IT Support Specialist
description:
Description:

Chasm Opportunities LLC is a small technology consulting firm seeking a reliable, detail oriented web and IT support specialist for ongoing part-time work. This role focuses on:

•WordPress website maintenance,

 •Google Workspace administration for a 6-person team,

and light weekly IT troubleshooting. 

You must be comfortable working independently with a narrow scope of responsibilities.

Initial Priorities

• Migrate WordPress site to Hostinger

o Move the current WordPress website from a marketing agency's hosting to Hostinger

o Ensure all pages, contact forms, and basic tracking continue to work after migration

o Coordinate with Porkbun for all necessary DNS updates (A, CNAME, MX records)

• Fix and verify contact form routing

o Confirm the site's "Contact Me" form reliably sends submissions to the correct inbox

o Troubleshoot and resolve any delivery or spam routing issues

• Add a blog section to the WordPress site

o Set up a functional blog page with standard post formatting

o Ensure it integrates cleanly with the existing site design

• Resolve Google Workspace internal
 going to spam

o Diagnose why
s sent between tea
mbers (6 users) are landing in spam

o Implement proper SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records via Porkbun to resolve the issue

o Verify internal mail delivery is working correctly for all tea
mbers

•Ongoing Weekly Support (Avg. 2–4 hrs/week)

• WordPress maintenance — periodic contact form checks, content updates, and plugin/theme upkeep

• Google Workspace admin support — user management, aliases, groups, permissions, and configuration changes for the 6-person team

• General IT troubleshooting — resolve week-to-week issues as they arise 
 delivery, access problems, cloud storage questions, etc.)

• DNS and domain support — assist with any Porkbun domain/DNS changes as needed

• Security awareness — basic cybersecurity hygiene; familiarity with CMMC concepts is a long-term plus but not required now

Tech Stack (Familiarity Is a Plus)

• Google Workspace Business Standard – primary collaboration tool for a 6-person team

• WordPress to be hosted on Hostinger

• Porkbun – domain and DNS management

• 
, Notion, Calendly, Dropbox, Slack



Requirements

• Demonstrated WordPress site migration experience (between hosting providers)

• Strong Google Workspace Admin console skills — user accounts, groups, aliases, security settings

• Solid DNS and
 authentication knowledge: MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC

• Ability to diagnose and fix internal
 spam/delivery issues in Google Workspace

• Familiarity with Porkbun or similar domain registrars

• English level C1 or C2 (as listed on OnlineJobs.ph)

• Willingness to sign an NDA prior to starting work

Work Hours & Logistics

• Average 2–4 hours per week over any given month

• Some weeks may require zero hours; others may need 4–6 hours depending on tasks

• Flexible schedule with some overlap with US Central Time for occasional screenshare sessions

• You guide; I click — Super Admin access stays with me

• Payment via Wise

Growth Potential

Exceptional performance may lead to a broader role supporting IT needs for Chasm Opportunities' small network of startup clients (WordPress, Google Workspace, Outlook, domain management, and similar work).



How to Apply

Please send a message that includes:

1. Your experience migrating WordPress sites between hosts.

2. Your Google Workspace Admin experience — what tasks you've handled and for

how many users.

3. Your experience diagnosing internal
 spam issues in Google Workspace or

similar platforms.

4. Your familiarity with DNS and
 authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) — and

specifically with Porkbun if applicable.

5. Any experience with tools in our stack: 
, Notion, Calendly, Dropbox, or

Slack.

6. Your typical availability in Philippines time and preferred overlap hours with US

Central Time.  Clearly state that you understand this is an average of 2-4 hours per week.

7. Provide your hourly rate for this position.

title: FULL-STACK DEVELOPER — SAAS PRODUCT BUILD
description:
About the Product

Quartermaster is a SaaS platform built specifically for chiropractic practices. It connects to clients'

QuickBooks accounts, pulls weekly financial data, runs it through an AI engine (Claude API), and

generates personalized CFO advisory scripts — giving financial advisors everything they need to run

high-value weekly client calls.

The core app is already built: dashboard, P&L analysis, forecasting, CFO script viewer, action item

tracker, and admin panel. You are joining to own the codebase long-term — adding features,

connecting live APIs, and making it production-grade.

React +

TypeScript

Node.js /

Express

SQLite + Drizzle

ORM

Tailwind +

shadcn/ui Claude AI API QuickBooks API

What You Will Build

Connect QuickBooks Online API

Implement OAuth2 flow so each client connects their QB account. Pull weekly P&L; reports, AR aging,

and balance sheet data automatically.

Wire Up Google Ads &
 Ads 

Pull campaign spend, ROAS, and conversion data from both platforms and display them on the

marketing attribution dashboard.

Make AI Script Generation Production-Ready

The Claude API integration is scaffolded. You will test it with real data, handle edge cases, tune the

prompts, and add a regenerate/edit workflow.

Build User Authentication

JWT login so each chiropractic practice logs in and sees only their data. Admin view shows all

clients.

Weekly Automated Reports

Schedule a Monday morning cron job that pulls QB data, generates the CFO script, and sends a

summary
 to the practice owner.

Quartermaster CFO Platform — Job Posting Page 1

Ongoing Features & Improvements

Once live with first clients, you will add features based on user feedback, fix bugs, improve

performance, and help scale to more clients.

Requirements

MUST HAVE NICE TO HAVE

• 3+ years of full-stack JavaScript experience

• React (hooks, context, TypeScript)

• Node.js + Express API development

• REST API integration (OAuth2 flows)

• Git — can commit, branch, and PR cleanly

• Communicates clearly in English (written)

• Can work independently with minimal hand-holding

• Reliable internet + backup connection

• QuickBooks API or Intuit OAuth experience

• Drizzle ORM or any SQLite experience

• Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui component library

• Experience with AI/LLM API calls (Claude, OpenAI)

• Google Ads API or
 Marketing API

• Previous SaaS product experience

• Experience with Railway, Render, or Vercel

Why This Role Is Worth Your Time

Long-term stability

This is not a short project. We are building a product business. Once you prove yourself, you are the

developer for years, not months.

Real ownership

You will have full access to the codebase. No micro-management. You get a task, you ship it.

Grow with the business

As we add clients, revenue grows. Compensation reviews happen every 6 months. Performance is

rewarded.

Interesting work

You are connecting AI, accounting software, and ad platforms into one dashboard. Not a boring

CRUD app.

Flexible schedule

Core overlap hours: 9am–12pm EST. Outside that, work when you work best. Results matter, not

hours logged.

How to Apply

Quartermaster CFO Platform — Job Posting Page 2

Read this carefully. We will filter out anyone who does not follow these instructions.

Send your application message with ALL of the following:

1. Your resume or a link to your LinkedIn/portfolio.

2. Links to 2–3 projects you built (GitHub repos preferred, live sites also great). Briefly explain

what each one is.

3. Answer this question in 3–5 sentences: "Tell me about a time you integrated a third-party

API that had confusing documentation. What was the API, what was the challenge, and how

did you solve it?"

4. Include the word CHIROPRACTOR somewhere in your message. This tells us you read the

full posting. Applications without it will not be reviewed.

5. Your expected monthly rate in USD.

Applications that are generic copy-paste messages will be ignored. We read every application that follows

these steps.

What Happens After You Apply

A small, realistic task from the actual codebase. Paid at your hourly rate

Review (2–3 days) We review all applications that followed the instructions above.

Short Video Call (30

min)

Quick intro call. We will discuss the project, your experience, and

answer your questions.

Paid Test Task (3–5 hrs) regardless of outcome.

Offer If it is a great fit on both sides, we move to an offer and start date.

Questions? Include them in your application message. We look forward to meeting our next tea
mber.

title: Part-time Capcut / AI Video Editor with potential for more
description:

You will be making AI videos using capcut or the video editing software of your choice. The editing process is fairly simple and straightforward.  Anyone who is proficient in video editing should have no problem with the basic tasks such as:

* Cutting dead space in videos

* Adding overlay images on screen in the videos

* Minor effects such as dynamic zoom, cropping, etc.

* Producing b-roll content from voiceover and footage provided, having the ability to digest what is being said and pair up the audio with corresponding footage



You will be making AI images and animating them with tools like Higgsfield, Veo 3, Kling, and Eleven labs. Experience with these tools is a big plus, but not required you will be trained on them. The image and video prompting is fairly simple and straightforward. 



Please review a few examples of my content to ensure you are familiar and comfortable with the editing style:

==

==



You will be working hand in hand with me on extremely viral social media pages and there is room for bonuses if deadlines are met and the content performs well!



Requirements:

* Attention to detail

* STRONG English

* Strong internet connection

* Previous experience with Capcut / TikTok / Instagram

* Good working with a team

* Ability to learn and take constructive feedback

* Availability to work 3-4h a day (with potential for more and increased salary)

$300/month + bonuses and potential full time work in the future with a salary increase.

title: Real Estate Cold Caller / Appointment Setter (Must Provide Own Dialer)
description:
We are a high-producing real estate team based in South Florida looking for a high-energy, experienced Real Estate Cold Caller to join our operation. Your primary focus will be Calling  Leads 



We will provide you with high-quality data , but you must have and maintain your own dialer .  Your objective is to have conversations with homeowners to gauge their motivation to sell, and then set a follow-up appointment for our lead agent.

Your Responsibilities:



Make 150–200 outbound dials per shift using your own dialer, using  proven scripts.



Identify homeowners who are considering selling in the next 6 months or wanting refinance mortgage



Set a minimum of 2 qualified listing appointment or warm follow-up call per shift.



Requirements:



Mandatory Software: You MUST have your own active subscription to a dialer. 

We will provide the lists, but we will not be providing or paying for your dialer platform.



Experience: You must have prior experience calling real estate leads for US-based agents/investors. 



Communication: Fluent English with a clear, neutral accent and excellent conversational skills. You must be comfortable handling objections without sounding like you are reading a script.



Equipment: A reliable, hard-wired high-speed internet connection, a quiet workspace, and a noise-canceling USB headset.

Hours & Compensation:



Pay: $4.00 to $5.00 USD plus bonus (depending on experience).



Schedule: Part-time, 20  hours per week.



Working Hours: You must be available to work from 4:00 PM to 7:40 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST), Monday through Friday.

Saturday - 11 am - 2pm 



How to Apply (READ CAREFULLY):

We are looking for someone with high attention to detail. If you do not follow these exact steps, your application will be instantly deleted.



Reply to this job posting with the exact subject line: "South Florida Caller  (I Have My Own Dialer)"



In your message, provide a brief summary of your real estate cold calling experience and explicitly state which dialer platform you currently use.



Audio Requirement: You must include a link to a voice recording . In the recording, introduce yourself and read the following script in your normal speaking voice:



"Hi, I’m calling for Keisha's real estate team. We recently helped a neighbor sell their home just down the street for a great price, and we still have buyers looking in the area. Have you considered taking an offer on your home while the market is this active?"

title: General Ecom VA  - Video Editor + Meta Ad launch
description:
About This Role



You'll be the person responsible for daily video content production at scale. We handle the strategy and tools — you handle the execution. Your job is simple: download videos, use our AI tools to edit them quickly, and upload them to our ad platform.

This is not creative work. This is execution work. We provide everything you need (tools, guidelines, competitor sources). You follow the process, hit daily targets, and the volume speaks for itself.

If you're reliable, fast, and comfortable with repetitive high-volume work, this is a good fit.



Your Workflow (Repeating Cycle - Daily)



Research & Download



We provide you with specific keywords and competitor sources

You find and download trending videos from TikTok matching those criteria

Daily target: 50-150 high-quality video files  (we have AI tools to help with this)







Edit & Brand



We provide AI tools to speed up the editing process

Apply our editing guidelines (branding, framing, formatting)

Keep edits minimal and consistent

Maintain consistent quality standards across batches

Daily target: 50-100 edited videos ready for upload (we have AI tools to help with this)





Upload & Launch



Add videos to
 Ads Manager in our accounts

Set up basic ad campaigns with provided budgets

Daily target: Videos live and tracking within 24 hours of download

(You will not manage the ads just launching them)



Track & Report



Monitor performance metrics (CPA, CTR, conversion rate)

Log results in shared spreadsheet/dashboard

Flag top performers and underperformers

Weekly: Performance reports and trend analysis









Core Skills Required

Must Have:



TikTok video downloading (yt-dlp, 4K Video Downloader, or equivalent) ( we have our own tool that we developed)

Video editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, Shotcut, or similar)

 Ads Manager experience

Google Sheets or Airtable proficiency

Attention to detail & organization (file naming, folder structure)

Comfortable learning new tools quickly



Nice to Have:



Ad copy/headline writing experience

A/B testing knowledge

Analytics/reporting experience

Graphic design basics

Motion graphics or effects editing





What Success Looks Like



Week 1-2: Understand our editing standards and workflow

Week 2-3: Producing 40-50 edited videos per day (we will provide AI tools to help you)

Ongoing: Consistently delivering 50+ videos/day with <2% quality issues

Month 2+: Identifying trends in what performs best, suggesting optimizations





Workflow Specifics



Turnaround: Download ? Edit ? Upload & Launch the ads within one business day

Daily volume: 40-50 videos minimum

Video length: Varies by test (we'll specify)

Editing complexity: Minimal (branding, formatting, framing) — AI tools provided

Ad manager setup: Simple (we provide templates/guidelines)

Reporting: Daily summary, weekly deep-dive (1-2 hrs)

Time commitment: ~3-4 hours/day once ramped up





Tools You'll Use



Download: yt-dlp, 4K Video Downloader, or similar ( we have our own AI tools we built for that)

Edit: Your choice of Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, or equivalent

Upload:
 Ads Manager

Track: Google Sheets, Airtable, or Supermetrics

Communicate: Slack (daily standups)





What We Provide



Detailed editing guidelines & templates

Competitor/keyword briefs (you don't have to source)

 Ads Manager access (you don't manage budget)

Clear feedback & iteration process

Flexible schedule (as long as turnaround is 48hrs)

Our own AI tools that we developed





Red Flags (What Doesn't Work)



You need detailed creative direction on every task

You can't commit to 7-9 hours daily (Monday-Friday)

You're uncomfortable with repetitive, high-volume work

You need to understand the "why" before executing tasks

You expect complex, varied creative work. start your application with a word blue.





How to Apply

Share:



Your portfolio (examples of videos you've edited or campaigns you've managed)

One paragraph: Why you want this role + what tools you're most comfortable with

Availability: Can you start in [DATE]? How many hrs/week can you commit?

Please share your WhatsApp and available hours. (Expect our team to reach out to you)



TRIAL: We will hire you for a trial for 2 hours to see your performance.

title: Technical Virtual Assistant / Automation Support (STEM Grad)

title: Virtual Assistant, Real Estate Photography & Digital Media
description:
GENERAL PURPOSE OF JOB

Wheeler Home Concepts is a premium real estate photography and marketing company serving Delaware and the surrounding areas (Eastern Standard Time, United States). We specialize in high-quality photography, video, digital media, and design services for realtors and businesses. Our reputation is built on excellence, creativity, and top-tier service.



We’re looking for a Virtual Assistant to support our growing team with photo editing quality control, virtual staging, reels editing, social media design, and design-related projects. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, highly organized, and skilled in creative software tools.



Please submit a portfolio of your professional work.



ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES include the following. Other duties may be assigned.



Photography & Media Support

• Quality Control (QC) for Photo Editing: Review edited photos to ensure consistency, quality, and adherence to brand standards. Make additional edits to photos, as needed.

• Virtual Staging: Assist in staging vacant rooms digitally using Photoshop or similar tools.

• Photobooth Templates: Design and update photobooth overlays and templates, as needed.

• Reels Editing: Create short-form video content (Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts) using Premier Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve.

• Flyers & Social Post Design: Design promotional materials and social media graphics.

• Assist with Writing and Maintaining Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs): Document processes and workflows to improve efficiency.

• Organization: Maintain organized records and files of images, projects, and updates in our system.



Design & Creative Assistance

• 3D Modeling/Renders for Design Projects: Use Planner 5D (or similar software) to create visual models for design projects, as needed.

• Assistance with Design Presentations: Help with presentations for clients and internal design projects.



Required Skills & Experience

• 2+ years of demonstrated experience with:

     o Adobe Lightroom (Photo Editing & QC)

Hi, I’m Luke, the founder of Tahanan.



Tahanan is a Tagalog learning app built for real progress in speaking, confidence, and cultural connection. Our strongest audience is heritage learners, especially people who grew up hearing Tagalog but never learned to speak it comfortably.



I’m looking for a small group of established tutors and creators who feel genuinely aligned with that mission.



This is a referral partner opportunity, not a tutoring job at Tahanan.

title: Remote Email Outreach Specialist — Fintech
description:
Email Setter



ClearHaven Advisory | Remote | Commission + $100/month retainer



ABOUT THE ROLE

ClearHaven is a US-based fintech advisory firm. We help international and domestic fintech companies get fully compliant and operational in the United States. We work with founders and executives at payments companies, neobanks, remittance platforms, and crypto-to-fiat businesses.



We are building our outbound
 team and looking for a sharp, disciplined
 outreach specialist. This is not a copy-paste
 blasting job. We send 100 highly researched, highly personalized
s per week to pre-qualified leads. Every
 is written to a specific person about a specific problem we found in our research. The people who do well in this role take pride in quality over volume.



REQUIREMENTS — PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING

You must have prior experience in cold
 outreach or B2B sales outreach. Applications without relevant experience will not be reviewed. You must also have a neutral or near-perfect English accent as this role includes cold calling warm leads by phone.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

-Every Friday: research 100 qualified leads using our lead database and qualification checklist. Find one specific buying trigger per person before you import them.

-Every Monday: send 100 personalized
s using our proven templates. Research is already done so this takes 3 to 4 minutes per
.

-Tuesday through Thursday: send follow-up sequences to prior weeks leads. Call warm leads who opened
s but did not reply.

-Respond to every positive reply within 1 hour and work the conversation until the call is on the calendar.

-Log all activity and notes in our CRM (GoHighLevel).



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

-Proven experience in cold
 outreach or B2B sales. You must be able to show examples or describe past campaigns.

-Near-perfect spoken and written English. Your
s and calls represent our company to senior fintech executives in the US market.

-Highly disciplined and organized. You manage your own week. Friday research has to be done before end of day without being chased.

-Comfortable doing online research. You will be looking up companies in public databases and finding specific information about each lead.

-Able to work independently. This is a remote role with no micromanagement. You are trusted to hit your numbers.



COMPENSATION

$100 per month retainer plus 5% commission on every closed deal your leads generate. Our deals range from $4,000 to $50,000 in value. A setter generating 1 to 2 closed deals per month can realistically earn $2,000 or more in monthly commission. There is no cap. Your income grows directly with your output.



TOOLS WE USE

Apollo, Yesware, Mailscale, Lusha, Dialdilar, GoHighLevel. Full training provided on all tools.



TO APPLY

Send a short message telling us about your cold
 or outreach experience. Include any results you can share — reply rates, calls booked, deals generated. Do not send a generic application. We read every message and we can tell immediately if you took 30 seconds or 3 minutes to write it. The people who get hired here pay attention to detail. Show us that in how you apply.

title: Experienced AI UGC content creator
description:
We’re looking for a creative and experienced AI UGC Content Creator with experience in Organic Marketing, who likes to create authentic and catching AI UGC content, with a keen eye to details while keeping the content simple, consistent and in high-quality.



Job description:



You’ll be using tools like Veo 3.1, Kling 2.6/3.0 video generation, Canva, CapCut, and ElevenLabs to create the content. You must have hands-on experience with these tools.



What you’ll do:



- Create high-quality visuals with Veo 3.1, Kling 3.0

- Edit short, engaging videos ready for posting

- Make AI-generated voiceovers using ElevenLabs

- Prepare polished, ready-to-publish content from start to finish



What we’re looking for:



- Real, hands-on experience with the tools above

- A creative mind with good visual and storytelling sense

- Availability to work any day from Monday to Sunday

- Strong attention to detail and the ability to make content that stands out

- fast and stable internet connection.



To apply, please send us your work portfolio in the message,



Thanks for understanding

title: Community Development Representative  Coron, Palawan
description:
We are hiring Community Development Representatives in five destinations across the

Philippines — Siargao, El Nido, Coron, Bohol and Panglao, and Boracay. This posting is for

Coron, Palawan.



This is not a typical sales role — and not a tech role either. The ideal person for this position is

part presenter, part community builder, part trusted local presence. You will be the face of this

effort — embedded in the hospitality and tourism community, genuinely invested in the success

of the people you work with, and comfortable introducing her to a community of business

owners who’ve never seen anything like her before.



Lani sells herself. Your job will be to get in front of people — and we will support you in that —

tell the story well, demonstrate something remarkable in real time, and build the kind of

relationships that make people want to say yes — and want to bring their friends along too.

This role comes with two possible growth paths. Some people are natural community builders

who thrive by going deeper in one territory — expanding their client base, strengthening their

community roots, and growing their income steadily over time. Others have leadership

ambitions and want to mentor new ambassadors, oversee multiple territories, and grow into a

regional role. Both paths are equally valued here. You choose the one that fits who you are

today — and that path can evolve as your life and experience develop.



What You Will Do

• Identify and attend local hospitality gatherings, tourism council meetings, DOT events,

restaurant association events, surf operator meetups, and industry get-togethers where

independent business owners congregate

• Deliver a compelling group presentation to rooms of boutique hotel owners, restaurant

operators, surf schools, and activity operators — live, interactive, and anchored in their

specific numbers — including a live demonstration of Lani being activated in real time in

front of the room

• Walk individual prospects through a one-on-one demonstration that shows the specific

revenue their business is currently leaving on the table — Lani does the heavy lifting,

your job is to start the conversation

• Build genuine relationships with independent business owners across the tourism

economy — before, during, and long after they become clients

• Ensure existing clients feel well served and are getting real value — staying present in

the relationship not just at the point of sale

• Introduce clients to the opportunity of joining a growing community of independent

operators — warmly and without pressure — and nurture that community as it develops

• Attend local hospitality and tourism events as a genuine community presence — not as

a vendor but as someone who belongs there

• Have regular conversations with the founder to share what you are learning and to get

support on whatever arises — whether it is a tough situation, an unexpected opportunity,

or simply something worth thinking through together

• Report weekly on conversations, feedback, and market insights

• Follow whichever growth path fits you — deepen your territory over time or grow into a

leadership role as the team expands across the Philippines



What We Are Looking For

Experience and background

We are looking for someone who already belongs to this community — or could walk into it

tomorrow and feel completely at home. The ideal background, in order of priority:



• First priority — hospitality and tourism: experience with boutique hotels, resorts, or

guesthouses as an owner, manager, or guest relations professional. You understand the

world of the independent hotel owner from the inside.

• Second priority — food and beverage: experience with restaurants, cafes, or bars in a

tourism destination. You know the pressures of running a small F&B operation and the

pain of missed reservations and unanswered messages.

• Third priority — surf schools and activity operators: familiarity with the surf and outdoor

activity community. You know the operators, the culture, and the seasonal rhythms.

• Also valued — dive shops and vacation rental operators. Any experience in these

industries is a genuine plus.

• Based in or willing to be deeply and consistently present in your destination

• Comfortable speaking in front of a group — you don’t need to be a professional

presenter but you need to be someone people enjoy listening to

• Existing relationships within the local business community is a significant advantage

• Comfortable with WhatsApp,
, and basic digital tools — you don’t need to be

technical but technology shouldn’t rattle you

• English proficiency — written and spoken



Personal qualities — these matter more than your resume

We don’t expect any candidate to embody all of these perfectly. But if most of them feel like a

genuine description of who you are, we would love to hear from you.

• Genuine warmth — people feel comfortable around you quickly

• A natural connector — you enjoy introducing people to each other and building

something larger than yourself

• Presence — when you speak people listen. Not because you’re loud but because you’re

real.

• Curiosity — you ask good questions and you actually listen to the answers

• Community pride — you care about the people who have built their lives and businesses

here

• Persistence — you follow up thoughtfully and keep relationships warm

• Integrity — you represent Lani honestly and let her results speak for themselves

• Eager to grow — in whatever direction feels right for you



Compensation

All figures at current exchange rates (~?57 per $1 USD).



Monthly Base salary

Guaranteed

?42,000

~$737



Travel and Starlink

?20,000

~$351



Total Monthly Base

?62,000

~$1088



Setup commissions

10% per client signed



These figures reflect realistic projections based on two group presentations per year across

multiple industries, a growing referral network, and an expanding client base. Your perpetual

residual income grows every year your book grows — and continues for as long as your clients

remain with Lani.



The perpetual residual is tiered to reward loyalty and long-term commitment:

• Clients 1 to 20 — 2% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 21 to 50 — 3% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 51 to 75 — 4% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 76 to 100 — 5% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients above 100 — 6% of monthly retainer per client

Your book belongs to you. The portfolio of clients you build is yours — value you can hold,

transfer, or one day retire on. The legal structure of book ownership is being developed with our

attorneys and will be fully documented before your first client signs.



How to Apply

Send a WhatsApp message or
 introducing yourself. Tell us:

• A little about your background and where you are based

• Why this role connects with you specifically — not just what you’ve done but what draws

you to this particular work in this particular community

• Tell us about a relationship — in your community, your work, or your personal life —

where both trust and genuine connection were part of the story. What made it real? And

how do you see the difference between the two?

We are not looking for a polished cover letter. We are looking for a real person who connects

with what we are building.

Most people can be trusted to some degree. Fewer people generate genuine connection. The

best relationships — and the best work — happen when both are present. Share your story with

us.

title: Social Media Manager
description:
Social Media Manager

5 Seasons Homestays - Property Management & Co-hosting

Location: Remote (Philippines-based preferred)

Schedule: Full-Time (40 hours/week)

Salary: $600 - $800 USD/month



About Us

We're a growing vacation rental property management company showcasing beautiful short-term rental properties. We need a creative Social Media Manager to build our brand, create engaging content, and drive bookings through social media.



What You'll Do

Create engaging content for Instagram,
, and TikTok

Transform property photos into dynamic videos using AI tools

Design and publish Instagram/Facebook Stories and Reels

Create and manage
 Ads campaigns to drive bookings

Develop and execute social media content calendar

Engage with followers and build online community

Analyze performance metrics and optimize strategy

Write compelling captions and ad copy





Required Skills

? 2+ years social media management experience (hospitality/travel/real estate preferred)

? AI photo-to-video conversion skills (Runway, Pika, D-ID, CapCut, or similar)

?
 Ads experience - proven track record with campaigns and ROI

? Instagram &
 expertise - Stories, Reels, content best practices

? Content creation - graphics, videos, engaging captions

? Excellent written English

? Self-starter - can work independently

? Full-time availability



Nice to Have

? GoHighLevel (GHL) platform experience

? TikTok content creation

? Graphic design (Canva, Adobe)

? Video editing skills

? Vacation rental/Airbnb social media experience



Compensation

$600 - $800/month (based on experience)



Benefits: Paid time off, holiday pay, flexible schedule, performance bonuses



To Apply - Submit ALL of the following:

1. Resume with social media experience and results

2. Portfolio (REQUIRED):

Links to social media accounts you've managed

Examples of AI-generated videos from photos

 Ads examples with performance metrics

Reels/Stories that performed well

Before/After growth metrics



3. Answer These Questions in a Loom video:

Q1: Which AI tools do you use to convert photos into videos? Describe your process.

Q2: Share a
 Ads campaign you ran - objective, creative, and results (CTR, cost per lead, ROAS).

Q3: You're launching a new luxury vacation rental. What's your 30-day social media strategy?

Q4: Have you used Go High Level? If yes, describe experience. If no, how quickly do you learn new platforms?





We need someone who can start ASAP. If you're creative, data-driven, and skilled with AI video tools and
 Ads, apply now!

title: Meta & Google Ads Specialist
description:
About Us:

We are a private acupuncture clinic focused on high-quality, patient-centered care. Our marketing approach is education-driven and designed to attract the right patients—not a high volume of low-quality leads.



We run a small number of focused campaigns and prioritize clarity, strategy, and long-term results over aggressive scaling.



What We’re Looking For:

We are looking for a Meta 
/Instagram) and Google Ads specialist who understands that success is not about generating the most leads, but the right leads.



This role is ideal for someone who prefers working thoughtfully with a smaller budget, refining strategy over time, and focusing on quality rather than volume.



You should be comfortable targeting a specific audience, avoiding low-intent leads, and contributing to overall messaging and strategy—not just managing campaigns.



Responsibilities:

Ad Strategy & Campaign Management

     Plan, launch, and manage Meta and Google Ads campaigns

     Work within a limited budget and scale only when performance justifies it

     Focus on lead quality and actual conversion outcomes, not vanity metrics



Targeting & Positioning

     Build highly specific audiences when appropriate

     Understand when to use broader targeting and guide the algorithm with strong inputs

     Align messaging with a premium, health-conscious audience

     Avoid attracting price-sensitive or low-intent leads



Creative Direction & Collaboration

     Work closely with our social media manager to develop aligned strategies

     Clearly communicate ad concepts, messaging, and angles

     Guide and collaborate on creative development (images, videos, copy)

     Ensure creatives support an education-first marketing approach



Performance Analysis

     Evaluate campaigns based on lead quality and consultation bookings

     Continuously refine targeting, messaging, and creative direction



Communication

     Provide clear, concise updates

     Be responsive and open to feedback

     Collaborate effectively with tea
mbers 



AI-Era Expectations:

     Understand how AI-driven ad systems (such as Meta Advantage+ and Google Performance Max) work

     Focus on improving inputs (creative, messaging, audience signals) rather than relying only on manual controls

     Be comfortable testing different messaging angles and learning from data

     Adapt to platform changes without relying on outdated tactics



What We Do NOT Want:

     Someone focused on generating cheap leads just to improve numbers

     Someone who only executes tasks without strategic thinking

     Someone who avoids involvement in creatives

     Someone who relies on outdated tactics or ignores feedback



Requirements:

     2–5+ years of hands-on experience with Meta Ads and Google Ads

     Experience working with small budgets

     Strong understanding of audience targeting and lead quality

     Ability to guide or contribute to ad creatives

     Strong communication and collaboration skills



We care more about how you think and make decisions than the number of years you’ve worked.



Job Details:



Part-time (ongoing)

Monthly pay: $400–$600 USD (based on experience)

Approximately 15–20 hours per week (flexible based on workload)

Opportunity for a performance-based bonus and compensation increase over time



This role is designed for long-term collaboration. As results improve, there will be opportunities to grow compensation and responsibility.



How to Apply:

     Please include:

          A short introduction (5–6 sentences max)

         1–2 examples of campaigns you managed (include budget, goal, and result)

         Your answer to this question:

         If your campaign is generating many cheap leads but low conversions, what would you do?

         Briefly explain how AI has changed your approach to running ads

          How you define a high-quality lead



Important:

     To confirm that you read this entire job post, please use the following subject line in your application:

     Quality Over Quantity – Ads Specialist

     Applications without this subject line will not be considered.

title: Marketing & Growth Specialist (Capital Raising / Investment Sales Focus)
description:
Part-Time / Full-Time (Fixed Monthly Retainer)

$500 - $1000



Job Overview:

I am looking for a strategic Marketing Specialist to help scale an investment sales operation. We focus on business owners and entrepreneurs who are looking to raise capital (PPMs, private equity, etc.). You will be responsible for completing our customer profiles, refining our website messaging, and executing outreach to high-level decision-makers.



Key Responsibilities:



Customer Persona Development: Finish our "Ideal Investor" and "Capital-Seeking Founder" profiles. You must understand the pain points of high-net-worth individuals.



Website Content & Conversion: Work with me to finalize website copy that builds authority. You should know how to turn a "visitor" into a "qualified lead."



High-Level Outreach: Manage LinkedIn and
 campaigns targeting business owners. No "spammy" tactics—we use high-authority, executive-style communication.



Zoho Integration: (Bonus) Assist in ensuring marketing leads flow correctly into our Zoho CRM/Books ecosystem.



Required Experience:



Proven track record in B2B Marketing or Investor Relations.



Experience writing for a sophisticated audience (Business owners, CEOs, Investors).



Ability to think strategically about a "funnel"—from first touch to a signed PPM.



Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator and CRM workflows.



How to Apply:



Start your message with "STRATEGIC GROWTH."



Provide a brief summary of any experience you have in the finance, investment, or real estate sectors.



Include a link to a writing sample or a website you have worked on.



Confirm your availability for a fixed monthly retainer.

title: Zoho Books Specialist ($100+/mo Fixed Retainer) – Setup & Expense Tracking
description:
am looking for a detail-oriented Zoho Books specialist to manage my business accounting. We have very low transaction volume, so I am looking for a fixed-price partnership rather than hourly billing. My primary goal is 100?curacy in expense tracking and a clean, automated setup.



Scope of Work (Fixed Monthly Deliverables):



Initial Cleanup (Month 1): Review and optimize the Chart of Accounts for investment sales/PPM structure. Sync all bank feeds.



Monthly Categorization: Ensure every single expense is categorized correctly (no "uncategorized" items).



Receipt Management: Monitor the Zoho Receipt inbox to ensure all digital receipts match the bank transactions.



Monthly Reconciliation: Reconcile all bank and credit card statements by the 5th of each month.



Monthly Report: Send a brief P&L summary and a "Missing Receipts" list once a month.



Requirements:



Expertise in Zoho Books is non-negotiable. (Please do not apply if you only know QuickBooks).



Experience setting up Bank Rules and Automated Workflows in Zoho.



Background in Accounting or Bookkeeping (CPA preferred but not required).



High attention to detail—you must be someone who catches the "small things."



How to Apply:



Start your application with the word "ZOHO EXPERT" so I know you read this.



Briefly describe your experience setting up Zoho Books from scratch.



Confirm that you are comfortable with a $100+/month (negotiate) fixed retainer for this low-volume role.

title: Job Title: Investment Sales Specialist – PPM & Financial Products
description:
ob Description

We are seeking a high-energy, experienced Investment Sales Specialist to join our team. This role is specifically for a professional who understands the nuances of Private Placement Memorandums (PPMs) and has a background in high-level financial sales.



If you have experience as a Stock Broker, Investment Consultant, or have successfully raised capital using PPMs, we want to hear from you. You will be responsible for communicating complex financial structures to potential investors and driving growth for our platform.



Key Responsibilities:



Consult with potential investors regarding our PPM offerings.



Manage the end-to-end sales cycle for sophisticated financial products.



Provide clear, compliant, and professional explanations of investment risks and rewards.



Maintain a high level of confidentiality and professionalism.



Requirements:



Proven track record in Financial Sales (Stock Brokerage, Private Equity, or similar).



Deep understanding of PPMs and private offering structures.



Exceptional English communication skills (written and verbal).



Ability to work independently and meet sales targets.



Suggested Settings for your Post:

Type of Employment: Choose Full Time or Part Time based on your current scale.



Wage/Salary: For this level of expertise, you might consider a base salary plus commission. A common starting range for specialized financial VAs is $800 – $1,200/month base, but you can click Suggest a Salary on the page to see current market rates.



Skills Requirement: 1.  Sales Representative

2.  Financial Analysis

3.  Investment Research



Pro-Tip: In the "Skills" search box on your screen, try searching for "Capital Raising" or "Investor Relations" to find candidates who specifically handle these high-value tasks.

Review the website

title: Community Development Representative  El Nido, Palawan
description:
We are hiring Community Development Representatives in five destinations across the

Philippines — Siargao, El Nido, Coron, Bohol and Panglao, and Boracay. This posting is for El

Nido, Palawan.

This is not a typical sales role — and not a tech role either. The ideal person for this position is

part presenter, part community builder, part trusted local presence. You will be the face of this

effort — embedded in the hospitality and tourism community, genuinely invested in the success

of the people you work with, and comfortable introducing her to a community of business

owners who’ve never seen anything like her before.

Lani sells herself. Your job will be to get in front of people — and we will support you in that —

tell the story well, demonstrate something remarkable in real time, and build the kind of

relationships that make people want to say yes — and want to bring their friends along too.



This role comes with two possible growth paths. Some people are natural community builders

who thrive by going deeper in one territory — expanding their client base, strengthening their

community roots, and growing their income steadily over time. Others have leadership

ambitions and want to mentor new ambassadors, oversee multiple territories, and grow into a

regional role. Both paths are equally valued here. You choose the one that fits who you are

today — and that path can evolve as your life and experience develop.



What You Will Do

• Identify and attend local hospitality gatherings, tourism council meetings, DOT events,

restaurant association events, surf operator meetups, and industry get-togethers where

independent business owners congregate

• Deliver a compelling group presentation to rooms of boutique hotel owners, restaurant

operators, surf schools, and activity operators — live, interactive, and anchored in their

specific numbers — including a live demonstration of Lani being activated in real time in

front of the room

• Walk individual prospects through a one-on-one demonstration that shows the specific

revenue their business is currently leaving on the table — Lani does the heavy lifting,

your job is to start the conversation

• Build genuine relationships with independent business owners across the tourism

economy — before, during, and long after they become clients

• Ensure existing clients feel well served and are getting real value — staying present in

the relationship not just at the point of sale

• Introduce clients to the opportunity of joining a growing community of independent

operators — warmly and without pressure — and nurture that community as it develops

• Attend local hospitality and tourism events as a genuine community presence — not as

a vendor but as someone who belongs there

• Have regular conversations with the founder to share what you are learning and to get

support on whatever arises — whether it is a tough situation, an unexpected opportunity,

or simply something worth thinking through together

• Report weekly on conversations, feedback, and market insights

• Follow whichever growth path fits you — deepen your territory over time or grow into a

leadership role as the team expands across the Philippines



What We Are Looking For

Experience and background

We are looking for someone who already belongs to this community — or could walk into it

tomorrow and feel completely at home. The ideal background, in order of priority:

• First priority — hospitality and tourism: experience with boutique hotels, resorts, or

guesthouses as an owner, manager, or guest relations professional. You understand the

world of the independent hotel owner from the inside.



• Second priority — food and beverage: experience with restaurants, cafes, or bars in a

tourism destination. You know the pressures of running a small F&B operation and the

pain of missed reservations and unanswered messages.

• Third priority — surf schools and activity operators: familiarity with the surf and outdoor

activity community. You know the operators, the culture, and the seasonal rhythms.

• Also valued — dive shops and vacation rental operators. Any experience in these

industries is a genuine plus.

• Based in or willing to be deeply and consistently present in your destination

• Comfortable speaking in front of a group — you don’t need to be a professional

presenter but you need to be someone people enjoy listening to

• Existing relationships within the local business community is a significant advantage

• Comfortable with WhatsApp,
, and basic digital tools — you don’t need to be

technical but technology shouldn’t rattle you

• English proficiency — written and spoken



Personal qualities — these matter more than your resume

We don’t expect any candidate to embody all of these perfectly. But if most of them feel like a

genuine description of who you are, we would love to hear from you.

• Genuine warmth — people feel comfortable around you quickly

• A natural connector — you enjoy introducing people to each other and building

something larger than yourself

• Presence — when you speak people listen. Not because you’re loud but because you’re

real.

• Curiosity — you ask good questions and you actually listen to the answers

• Community pride — you care about the people who have built their lives and businesses

here

• Persistence — you follow up thoughtfully and keep relationships warm

• Integrity — you represent Lani honestly and let her results speak for themselves

• Eager to grow — in whatever direction feels right for you



Compensation

All figures at current exchange rates (~?57 per $1 USD).



Monthly Base salary

Guaranteed

?42,000

~$737



Travel and Starlink

?20,000

~$351



Total Monthly Base

?62,000

~$1088



Setup commissions

10% per client signed



These figures reflect realistic projections based on two group presentations per year across

multiple industries, a growing referral network, and an expanding client base. Your perpetual

residual income grows every year your book grows — and continues for as long as your clients

remain with Lani.



The perpetual residual is tiered to reward loyalty and long-term commitment:

• Clients 1 to 20 — 2% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 21 to 50 — 3% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 51 to 75 — 4% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients 76 to 100 — 5% of monthly retainer per client

• Clients above 100 — 6% of monthly retainer per client

Your book belongs to you. The portfolio of clients you build is yours — value you can hold,

transfer, or one day retire on. The legal structure of book ownership is being developed with our

attorneys and will be fully documented before your first client signs.



How to Apply

Send a WhatsApp message or
 introducing yourself. Tell us:

• A little about your background and where you are based

• Why this role connects with you specifically — not just what you’ve done but what draws

you to this particular work in this particular community

• Tell us about a relationship — in your community, your work, or your personal life —

where both trust and genuine connection were part of the story. What made it real? And

how do you see the difference between the two?

We are not looking for a polished cover letter. We are looking for a real person who connects

with what we are building.

Most people can be trusted to some degree. Fewer people generate genuine connection. The

best relationships — and the best work — happen when both are present. Share your story with

us.
